--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-04-09) ---
[2018-04-09 19:51] acoopa (Cooper): But thermite is an old unit, no?  So why is absorb only now dead
[2018-04-09 19:52] .bky_1556 (bky): Old thermite was too expensive to buy enough to deny absorb for an extended time.
[2018-04-09 19:53] wilson0862 (Wilson): Thermite was changed. Now you get to put back twice as much damage a turn. It's more feasible to stay on 8 damage than it is to stay on 4, for one.
[2018-04-09 19:53] .bky_1556 (bky): Now, you can deny absorb up to 8 permanent attack.
[2018-04-09 19:54] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): is thermite still relevant in the situation where your attack is high enough?
[2018-04-09 19:54] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): forcing your opponent to buy some amount of soak
[2018-04-09 19:54] darkeisbein: it is still relevant in that it gives you offesnive finesse
[2018-04-09 19:54] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): since 1 pt of soak roughly equals 2 gold
[2018-04-09 19:55] darkeisbein: basically if you get less damage after absorb then you would get pixies by clicking thermites
[2018-04-09 19:55] darkeisbein: clicking thermites is good
[2018-04-09 19:56] darkeisbein: also you can potentially use it to set up for frontline kills
[2018-04-09 19:56] .bky_1556 (bky): Even if you have exactly 9 attack, clicking 4 Thermites is usually a profit.
[2018-04-09 19:56] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): say situation: it's a DGrid game and i have ~20 damage. i bought a few Thermite here and there, and hardly ever clicked it because my damage is enough to force my opponent to buy a good amount of soak every turn
[2018-04-09 19:56] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): i understand the strength of thermite in the situations where your attack barely goes over your opponent's biggest absorber
[2018-04-09 19:56] .bky_1556 (bky): Then you probably don't want to buy more thermite, but if you have one...
[2018-04-09 19:57] darkeisbein: thermite is at it's best if you do 0 damage a turn
[2018-04-09 19:57] .bky_1556 (bky): ...you should use it when you can target engineers with it.
[2018-04-09 19:58] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): what's the difference between clicking thermite and killing engies? arent they equal (given that your attack is well above your opponent's biggest absorber)
[2018-04-09 19:58] .bky_1556 (bky): If you kill an engineer rather than a wall, it forces your opponent to defend slightly less efficiently by making them replace their granularity.
[2018-04-09 19:58] .bky_1556 (bky): If they do not, you have pixies now to punish them.
[2018-04-09 19:59] darkeisbein: basically against an enemy wall using thermites to get rid of all your attack gives you 2 attack a turn
[2018-04-09 19:59] darkeisbein: against bigger absorbers it gives more
[2018-04-09 20:00] p0lari (Polari): bucky is saying you want to click thermite/pixies if it means killing more engis, not that you want to click when otherwise killing engis
[2018-04-09 20:00] p0lari (Polari): for example 10 damage vs 4 engis and 2 energy matrices would be a great time to click a thermite
[2018-04-09 20:00] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): oh ok, that's what i thought as well
[2018-04-09 20:00] darkeisbein: basically for pixie clicks priorities are breach > exploit > killing granularity (a.k.a. killing engies)
[2018-04-09 20:01] p0lari (Polari): but you really shouldn't be buying thermite unless you're planning to spend (almost) all your damage to deny absorb
[2018-04-09 20:01] p0lari (Polari): "bought a few Thermite here and there" sounds like a mistake
[2018-04-09 20:01] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): i wouldve sunk the blue and spare 1 gold anyways
[2018-04-09 20:01] .bky_1556 (bky): Also, if you otherwise have no granular attack, you should consider clicking your last thermite the turn it expires.
[2018-04-09 20:01] darkeisbein: unless you are doing something specific like denying a T-head with it
[2018-04-09 20:01] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): thats why i bought the thermites that later proved to be irrelevant
[2018-04-09 20:02] p0lari (Polari): 1 gold isn't irrelevant though
[2018-04-09 20:02] .bky_1556 (bky): NB: a thermite isn't quite irrelevant if it makes your opponent buy an engineer early for granularity! Still not worth 1 gold, but...
[2018-04-09 20:03] p0lari (Polari): also wasting blue is usually a sign you were overteched
[2018-04-09 20:04] .bky_1556 (bky): In the extreme case where you would otherwise waste B AND it forces your opponent to defend with 5 engineers instead of an Aegis, it actually does come out a bit ahead even if you never click
[2018-04-09 20:04] p0lari (Polari): unless it was for some high tech unit early
[2018-04-09 20:04] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): DGrid game, had to get 3 :Blastforge:s
[2018-04-09 20:05] .bky_1556 (bky): In a DGrid game, you typically should get a Thermite Core early enough that you will click it.
[2018-04-09 20:05] p0lari (Polari): I mean, sure, it's good if you do actually get the exploit value
[2018-04-09 20:05] p0lari (Polari): but you hardly ever did with the *old* thermite, which was better at that
[2018-04-09 20:06] .bky_1556 (bky): Even with old thermite, the value of forcing higher granularity defense wasn't negligible.
[2018-04-09 20:06] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): http://play.prismata.net/?r=UTYUO-MvdOR
[2018-04-09 20:08] .bky_1556 (bky): You should have bought that thermite core a turn earlier.
[2018-04-09 20:09] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): now that i look back at that game i made a lot of glaring mistakes :NotLikeThis:
[2018-04-09 20:09] .bky_1556 (bky): Even given the turn you got it, you should have clicked it next turn, along with Manticore.
[2018-04-09 20:09] .bky_1556 (bky): I thought you said you never clicked it.
[2018-04-09 20:10] p0lari (Polari): the first mistakes are not getting third engi and getting defense grid against 1 damage
[2018-04-09 20:10] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): i hardly ever clicked it later in the game; i did click my manticores and thermites when my damage was low
[2018-04-09 20:10] p0lari (Polari): and there's no way wasting blue is correct with manticore in set
